summary: Treewide whitespace cleanup to comply with an upcoming .editorconfig check. The PR body says the cleanup was applied with `eclint -fix` and explicitly ignored `tools/{m2c,calcprogress}/` because those directories are subtrees. No changed-file list or diff excerpt was present in the provided slice, so exact touched files and formatting edits cannot be enumerated.
what_changed: Fixed whitespace treewide for consistency with an upcoming `.editorconfig` check.
Excluded `tools/{m2c,calcprogress}/` from the cleanup because those directories are subtrees.
The provided PR slice reports zero changed files and no diff excerpt, so the exact edits are not available from this context.
smart_moves: Used an automated formatter/linter command, `eclint -fix`, rather than manual whitespace edits, which reduces accidental semantic changes.
Avoided modifying vendored/subtree-style tool directories, specifically `tools/m2c/` and `tools/calcprogress/`, preserving external subtree integrity.
Landed the cleanup ahead of enforcing `.editorconfig`, reducing future CI or review noise from whitespace-only violations.
decomp_lessons: For repository-wide style normalization, prefer automated tooling and document the exact command used in the PR body.
Do not run treewide formatters blindly over subtree, vendored, or external-tool directories; explicitly exclude them when they should remain byte-for-byte or upstream-compatible.
Whitespace-only cleanups can be useful infrastructure work before enabling stricter formatting checks, but they should be isolated from semantic decompilation changes.
review_feedback: No review comment text was included in the slice. The metadata reports one review, but no review comments or examples were provided.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that introduced or enforced `.editorconfig` checks after PR #647.
Search for later whitespace-only cleanup PRs that used `eclint -fix`.
Check whether `tools/m2c` and `tools/calcprogress` are consistently excluded from repository-wide formatting or tooling changes.
